summary: Test suite has good coverage of core happy-path functionality (glob matching, priority ordering, deduplication) but critical flaws undermine its trustworthiness. Major issues: (1) Violates explicit requirement that ALL TESTS MUST RUN THROUGH ACT—pr-label-assigner.bats runs directly with bats, not through workflow; workflow-structure.bats explicitly runs OUTSIDE act. (2) Only 3 act-based test cases (run-act-tests.sh), which is minimal. (3) Missing error-case testing: no tests for invalid priorities, malformed rules, or ? glob character despite code supporting these. (4) Inconsistent architecture: bats for direct tests, custom bash for act tests. (5) Brittle workflow tests using grep assertions on YAML. Would catch basic script bugs but fail to validate workflow integration and error handling.
